Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its scale and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == Dimensions == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |Alternate name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun |Group = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |Coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its scale and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == Dimensions == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |Alternate name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun |Group = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Dimensions = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its scale and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == Dimensions == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center.}} == Cultural Properties == === Katori City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Historic Site''' - Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (designated June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == Dimensions == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |Alternate name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun |Group = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center.}} == Cultural Properties == === Katori City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Historic Site''' - Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (designated June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. revision.comment: another mass edit to try to fix parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun |Group = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center.}} == Cultural Properties == === Katori City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Historic Site''' - Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (designated June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': - Diameter: 68 meters - Height: 9.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': - Width: 62 meters - Height: 7.5 meters - Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-typed kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-typed kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-typed tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-typed examples, were found on the mound. A shield-typed moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center.}} == other_designation == === Katori City Designated Cultural Property === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (designated June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits artifacts from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (formerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to form the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was designated a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the former Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus dimensions are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to form part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls.
