Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Otsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |Cultural Property = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Otsukayama Kofun (other_designation1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City Cultural Property Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * other_designation explanatory board (Katori City Board of Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** Structure: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == other_designation == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''other_designation''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (other_designation1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * other_designation explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == other_designation == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''other_designation''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (other_designation1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * other_designation explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |other_designation = Katori City Designated other_designation: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was other_designation1 a Katori City other_designation in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City other_designation on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == Historic Site == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Historic Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic Site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 Archive of Katori Heritage vol.078: Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun] - Katori City Homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sannobunme Ōtsukayama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Katori]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Katori City Historic Sites]] [[Category:San-no-fun-me Ōtsukayama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sannobunme Ōtsukayama Kofun]] [[ja:三ノ分目大塚山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}}
