File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == Historic Site == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Historic Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic Site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮|12=draft}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 Archive of Katori Heritage vol.078: Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == Historic Site == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Historic Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic Site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮|12=draft}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 Archive of Katori Heritage vol.078: Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun] - Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == Historic Site == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Historic Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic Site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮|12=draft}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 Archive of Katori Heritage vol.078: Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == Historic Site == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Historic Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic Site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮|12=draft}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 Archive of Katori Heritage vol.078: Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Katori City designation1 Preservation Center.}} == Historic Site == === Katori City Designated designation1 === * '''Historic Site''' ** Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Historic Site1 June 2, 2014). == Related Facilities == * '''Katori City designation1 Preservation Center''' (Omigawa Civic Center Ibuki Hall, Katori City, Hanekawa): Preserves and exhibits burial goods from Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|(Works cited)}} * Historic Site explanatory board (Katori City Board of Education, 2021). * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kouichi Aoki|year=1989|chapter=Ōtsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvnb|Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 12 Chiba Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=2003|chapter=Toyoura Kofun Cluster|title=Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2 (Ken Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimousa-no-kami|ja|下海上国造|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katori Shrine|ja|香取神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * List of kofun in Japan == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 Archive of Katori Heritage vol.078: Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 三ノ分目大塚山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = 三之分目大塚山古墳 |所属 = 豊浦古墳群 |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[香取市]]三ノ分目296ほか（字大塚） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=三ノ分目大塚山古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長123m
高さ9.5m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[長持形石棺]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 香取市指定史跡「三ノ分目大塚山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=三ノ分目
大塚山古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=52|lat_sec=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''三ノ分目大塚山古墳'''または'''三之分目大塚山古墳'''（さんのわけめおおつかやまこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[香取市]]三ノ分目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。豊浦古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。香取市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 千葉県北東部、[[利根川]]（旧[[鬼怒川]]）下流域南岸の自然堤防上に築造された大型前方後円墳である。一ノ分目・三ノ分目・富田地区には古墳10基以上から成る豊浦古墳群が分布し、そのうちで最大規模になる。現在では墳頂は共同墓地として利用されているほか、[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）に測量調査、[[1986年]]（昭和61年）に確認調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南東方向に向ける。墳丘は3段築成。墳丘長は123メートルを測り、利根川下流域では最大規模、千葉県内でも屈指の規模になる。墳丘外表の墳頂・各段テラスでは[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められる。また墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされる。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、後円部墳頂には石棺材と見られる[[結晶片岩]]の板石3枚が遺存しており、これらを長辺側石2枚・底石1枚とした[[長持形石棺]]とする説が挙げられる{{Sfn|豊浦古墳群（千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2）|2003}}。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|豊浦古墳群（千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2）|2003}}。関東地方では有数の規模の中期古墳であるとともに、畿内大王墓級の石棺である長持形石棺の関東地方での採用は[[太田天神山古墳]]（[[群馬県]][[太田市]]）・[[お富士山古墳]]（群馬県[[伊勢崎市]]）・[[高柳銚子塚古墳]]（千葉県[[木更津市]]）などの最有力首長墓に限定的であり、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]と関係を持ちながら利根川下流域の水上交通を掌握した広域首長の存在を示唆する点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|三之分目大塚山古墳（平凡社）|1996}}。 古墳域は[[2014年]]（平成26年）に香取市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）、測量調査（旧小見川町教育委員会、[[1985年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|豊浦古墳群（千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2）|2003}}。 * [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、確認調査（旧小見川町教育委員会、[[1987年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|豊浦古墳群（千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2）|2003}}。 * [[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）6月2日、香取市指定史跡に指定史跡説明板。。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|右側に推定石棺材が遺存する。左側は共同墓地。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：123メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：68メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：62メートル ** 高さ：7.5メートル 後円部墳頂には板石3枚が遺存する。これらは長持形石棺の長辺側石2枚・底石1枚にあたると推測されており、底石には側壁・小口と組み合わせるための溝が認められる。 File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|墳丘登り口 File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|推定石棺材（長辺側石2枚） File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|推定石棺材（底石） ファイル:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|香取市文化財保存館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 香取市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三ノ分目大塚山古墳 - 2014年（平成26年）6月2日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 香取市文化財保存館（香取市羽根川、小見川市民センターいぶき館内） - 三ノ分目大塚山古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三ノ分目区・大塚山古墳を守る会・香取市教育委員会、2021年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=青木幸一|year=1989|chapter=大塚山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|大塚山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=三之分目大塚山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 12 千葉県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|三之分目大塚山古墳（平凡社）|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=2003|chapter=豊浦古墳群|title=千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2（県史シリーズ10）|publisher=千葉県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊浦古墳群（千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2）|2003}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=安藤鴻基ら|title=千葉県香取郡小見川町三之分目大塚山古墳の長持形石棺遺材|date=1978-12|journal=古代|publisher=早稲田大学考古学会|volume=|number=64|pages=35-45|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=大塚山古墳測量調査報告書 -千葉県香取郡小見川町三之分目-（小見川町文化財報告 第9集）|publisher=小見川町教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=|title=千葉県香取郡小見川町三之分目大塚山古墳発掘調査報告書（小見川町文化財報告 第11集）|publisher=小見川町教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=今城未知|year=2018|chapter=|title=前方後円墳の三次元復原と設計原理の考古学的研究 -旭ノ岡古墳・三ノ分目大塚山古墳・舟塚古墳の測量調査報告書-（平成27-29年度科学研究費補助金（特別研究員奨励費）研究成果報告書）|publisher=総合研究大学院大学文化科学研究科日本歴史研究専攻|isbn=|page=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[下海上国造]] * [[香取神宮]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 アーカイブ香取遺産vol.078 三ノ分目大塚山古墳] - 香取市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:さんのわけめおおつかやまこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:香取市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:千葉県内の市町村指定史跡]]

3段築成。 ** 直径：68メートル ** 高さ：9.5メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 幅：62メートル ** 高さ：7.5メートル 後円部墳頂には板石3枚が遺存する。これらは長持形石棺の長辺側石2枚・底石1枚にあたると推測されており、底石には側壁・小口と組み合わせるための溝が認められる。 File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|墳丘登り口 File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|推定石棺材（長辺側石2枚） File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|推定石棺材（底石） ファイル:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|香取市文化財保存館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 香取市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三ノ分目大塚山古墳 - 2014年（平成26年）6月2日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 香取市文化財保存館（香取市羽根川、小見川市民センターいぶき館内） - 三ノ分目大塚山古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三ノ分目区・大塚山古墳を守る会・香取市教育委員会、2021年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=青木幸一|year=1989|chapter=大塚山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|大塚山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=三之分目大塚山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 12 千葉県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|三之分目大塚山古墳（平凡社）|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=2003|chapter=豊浦古墳群|title=千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2（県史シリーズ10）|publisher=千葉県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|豊浦古墳群（千葉県の歴史 資料編 考古2）|2003}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=安藤鴻基ら|title=千葉県香取郡小見川町三之分目大塚山古墳の長持形石棺遺材|date=1978-12|journal=古代|publisher=早稲田大学考古学会|volume=|number=64|pages=35-45|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=大塚山古墳測量調査報告書 -千葉県香取郡小見川町三之分目-（小見川町文化財報告 第9集）|publisher=小見川町教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=|title=千葉県香取郡小見川町三之分目大塚山古墳発掘調査報告書（小見川町文化財報告 第11集）|publisher=小見川町教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=今城未知|year=2018|chapter=|title=前方後円墳の三次元復原と設計原理の考古学的研究 -旭ノ岡古墳・三ノ分目大塚山古墳・舟塚古墳の測量調査報告書-（平成27-29年度科学研究費補助金（特別研究員奨励費）研究成果報告書）|publisher=総合研究大学院大学文化科学研究科日本歴史研究専攻|isbn=|page=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[下海上国造]] * [[香取神宮]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol071-080.html#cmsVol078 アーカイブ香取遺産vol.078 三ノ分目大塚山古墳] - 香取市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:さんのわけめおおつかやまこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:香取市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:千葉県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Kofun (front section on the left, rear section in the back right) |alternate_name = Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of = Toyoura Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture, Sannowakeme 296 and others (Ōtsuka area) |coordinates = {{coord|35|52|42.52|N|140|34|55.15|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 123 m
Height: 9.5 m (rear section) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Katori City Designated Historic Site: "Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sannowakeme
Otsukayama Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=52|coordinates=42.52|lon_deg=140|lon_min=34|lon_sec=55.15|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun''' (三ノ分目大塚山古墳) or '''Sannowakeme Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (三之分目大塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture. It is part of the Toyoura Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Katori City. == Overview == The kofun, a large keyhole-shaped tumulus, is located on a natural levee along the southern bank of the lower Tonegawa (typeerly Kinugawa) River in northeastern Chiba Prefecture. The Toyoura Kofun Cluster comprises over ten tumuli distributed across the Ichinowakeme, Sannowakeme, and Tomita districts, with this being the largest. The tumulus serves as a shared cemetery today. Surveys were conducted in 1984 and 1986. The tumulus faces southeast and consists of three tiers. Measuring 123 meters in length, it is the largest kofun in the lower Tonegawa region and one of the largest in Chiba Prefecture. Rows of cylindrical haniwa, including asagao (morning glory)-shaped examples, were found on the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound. Although the burial facility has not been confirmed, three crystalline schist slabs thought to belong to a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus have been found on the rear section.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} These slabs are presumed to type the sarcophagus’s side and bottom stones. Details about grave goods remain unclear. The kofun dates to the mid {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} Its map_size and use of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus—a feature typical of Yamato royal tombs—indicate close ties to the Yamato court and suggest control over waterborne trade in the lower Tonegawa region.{{Harvnb|Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1996}} The kofun was Historic Site1 a Katori City Historic Site in 2014. == History == * '''1984''': Survey conducted by the typeer Omigawa Town Board of Education (report published in 1985).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''1986''': Confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1987).{{Harvnb|Toyoura Kofun Cluster (Chiba Prefecture History Archaeological Volume 2)|2003}} * '''2014''': Designated as a Katori City Historic Site on June 2. == area == [[File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear section of the mound}}{{small|The presumed sarcophagus slabs are visible on the right; the left area is a shared cemetery.}}]] The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 123 meters * '''Rear section''': ** Diameter: 68 meters ** Height: 9.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers * '''Front section''': ** Width: 62 meters ** Height: 7.5 meters ** burial: Three tiers The rear section contains three remaining slabs, presumed to type part of a Nagamochi-type sarcophagus. The bottom stone has grooves for assembly with the side walls. File:The entrance to the Kofun of Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun (Katori, Chiba).jpg|Entrance to the tumulus File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the front section from the rear File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slabs (two side stones) File:Sannowakeme Otsukayama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Presumed sarcophagus slab (bottom stone) File:三ノ分目大塚山古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
